prerequisites: A Zendesk QA (formerly Klaus) account with Admin access, since only Admins can create API connections and obtain API credentials.
An API token generated from a custom integration in Zendesk QA (maps to the required api_key field).
Your Klaus account ID (maps to the required account field), copied from the custom integration's connection settings.
Your Klaus workspace ID (maps to the required workspace field), found in the URL when viewing workspace settings.
Optionally, a start date to bound the data range (maps to the optional start_date field).

authMethods.name: API key (Bearer token)
authMethods.summary: Authentication uses a single API token supplied via the api_key field. Zendesk QA (Klaus) expects it as a Bearer token in the Authorization header (Authorization: Bearer <api_token>) against the API base at https://pub.klausapp.com. Admins generate the token by adding a custom integration in Zendesk QA settings.

setupSteps.title: Open Connections in Zendesk QA as an Admin
setupSteps.body: Sign in to Zendesk QA (formerly Klaus) with an Admin account. Click your profile icon and go to Settings, then under the Account section select Connections. API credentials can only be obtained by Admins.

setupSteps.title: Create a custom integration to generate the API token
setupSteps.body: Click Add custom integration, enter a name, select a retention period, and click Add. Locate the new integration and use the copy button to reveal and copy the API token. Use this value for the api_key field.

setupSteps.title: Copy your account ID
setupSteps.body: On the custom integration, open the options menu and select Edit connection to find and copy your account ID. Enter this value in the account field.

setupSteps.title: Find your workspace ID
setupSteps.body: Open the workspace settings in Zendesk QA and read the workspace ID from the page URL. Enter this value in the workspace field.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector
setupSteps.body: Provide the api_key (API token), account (account ID), and workspace (workspace ID). Optionally set start_date to limit the range of data synced. The connector authenticates against https://pub.klausapp.com using the token as a Bearer credential.
